meaning: n.[鍖籡(鐢靛嚮)鍘荤氦棰ゅ櫒
examples: Adapted in imaginative ways, many have reappeared on city streets and village greens housing tiny cafes, cellphone repair shops or even defibrillator machines.
His words were like a mood defibrillator.
He has taken CPR courses, and he's qualified to use an electric defibrillator.
Only one implantable-defibrillator patient in five ends up needing a jolt to the heart.
Instead of killing, however, Brock's on-screen character goes around reviving enemies with a defibrillator.
